title: No 'jjf delete' / 'jjf abandon' verb — mis-filed issues can only be closed
body:
# Symptom

There is no `jjf delete <id>` or `jjf abandon <id>` verb. If
you create an issue by mistake (typo title, wrong type, test
ticket), the only options are:

1. `jjf close <id>` — issue persists, clutters
   `jjf ls --status all` and any history queries.
2. `git update-ref -d refs/jjf/issues/<id>` — works but
   bypasses the storage layer's audit trail.

# Design call

Append-only history is a feature for an audit trail, so a hard
delete may be the wrong primitive. But mis-filed test issues
will accrete across 10 projects.

Three options:

1. **`jjf abandon <id>`** — sets a new status `abandoned`,
   omitted from `jjf ls` and `jjf ready` by default but visible
   under `--status all`. Audit-trail-friendly.
2. **`jjf delete <id>`** — hard removes the ref. Recoverable
   only via reflog. Loud confirmation prompt (`--yes` to skip).
3. **Status quo + docs** — document that close is the only
   primitive and explain the recovery path for accidents.

(1) feels right for this codebase's append-only ethos.

# Confidence

Verified via `jjf --help` — no delete/abandon verb exists.
